stocks.note: *Numbers as of market close on March 5th at 4:00 PM ET
stocks.news: Markets pulled back sharply on Wednesday as rising oil prices and renewed geopolitical concerns weighed on sentiment. The Dow led declines, dropping over 540 points (-1.13%), while the S&P 500 fell 0.56% to 6,830.71. The NASDAQ bucked the trend, gaining 1.03% to 22,748.99 as tech names held up. Investors remain cautious ahead of Friday's jobs report, which could set the tone for the Fed's next move.

economy.commentary:
The 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.17%, its highest level in about a month, as inflation fears intensified amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. Gold surged to a new intraday record of $5,420.00 per ounce — a remarkable move even as yields climbed — driven by stagflation hedging and safe-haven demand in the wake of the Iran conflict.

Silver traded at $30.85, as precious metals broadly reflected the market's growing appetite for defensive positioning. Analysts note that the unusual simultaneous rise in both gold and Treasury yields signals a potential "stagflationary" environment, with investors hedging against both inflation and economic slowdown.

crypto.commentary:
Bitcoin held relatively steady at $68,853, edging up 0.41% as it continues to consolidate above the $68K level. Ethereum, however, dropped sharply by 7.65% to $1,858 as selling pressure intensified amid broader risk-off sentiment. XRP slipped 2.11% to $1.39, pulling back from recent highs.

The crypto market is showing divergence, with Bitcoin acting as a relative safe haven within the asset class while altcoins face heavier selling. Analysts are watching the $67K support level for BTC as a key indicator of near-term direction.

real_estate.homebuilder: Congress is advancing the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, a major reform package aimed at boosting supply and streamlining construction, despite concerns from homebuilders regarding potential investor bans on single-family home purchases.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area spring market is showing clear acceleration as of March 2026, with rising inventory and selective strength across the region. Santa Clara County remains the hottest market in the Bay Area, with single-family homes averaging just 14 days on market and a tight 1.0-month supply despite broader regional cooling trends.

📍 Alameda County (Fremont Focus):
• Median Sale Price: $925,000 — a 2.4% increase year-over-year
• Homes Sold: Dipped 4.7% as buyers navigate higher interest rates
• Competition: Properties like 35100 King Ct in Fremont recently sold for 19% over list price
• Market Outlook: Entering the spring acceleration phase with selective strength

📍 Santa Clara County:
• Days on Market: Just 14 days for single-family homes
• Supply: Ultra-tight 1.0-month supply, keeping upward pressure on prices
• Key Trend: Tech-centric demand continues to drive resilience even as other regions see more moderate price adjustments

Latest General News in the Bay Area:

📰 Fremont: Local investigators clarified that a small fire on a bridge bike lane was not the cause of death for a dog found at the scene earlier this week.

📰 Milpitas Community Support: Community organizations have rallied to provide increased support for unhoused and immigrant residents following recent regional policy shifts. The Milpitas Rotary Foundation and local donors were highlighted for their "Milpitas Wishes" campaign to assist community members in need.

📰 Tri-Valley Jobs Fair: Residents in the Tri-Valley area are preparing for the upcoming Spring Job & Apprenticeship Fair scheduled for March 11.
